text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs use the JSON format, which organizes information in a simple key-value structure. Before accessing many APIs, you need authentication—often an API key—to prove you are allowed to make requests. It is also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can send in a given time. If you ignore these limits, the server may block you temporarily. Finally, always handle errors properly. Check the server's response for status codes and messages, and write your code to manage issues like network failures or invalid data. This helps build reliable applications.
